title: The One Where Open Source Fought Back

cards.headline: AI Milestones This Week in History

events.text: ELIZA chatbot created at MIT — the first conversational AI
AlexNet wins ImageNet — deep learning revolution begins
Meta releases Llama — open-source LLMs go mainstream
Llama 5 hits 4.7T parameters — open-source rivals closed models on every benchmark

cards.headline: 1 Million
cards.body: AI agents deployed in production worldwide — crossing the seven-figure milestone for the first time this month.
cards.source: a16z State of AI Agents Report, April 2026

cards.headline: Open Source vs Closed Source LLMs
cards.body: The gap has narrowed to just 3 points on MMLU-Pro. A year ago it was 18 points. Open source is catching up at an accelerating pace — and it's free.

cards.headline: AI Art Generator Creates 'Abstract Nothing'
cards.body: A company's AI image generator was asked to create a 'minimalist logo.' It generated a completely blank white canvas and titled it 'The Essence of Simplicity.' The design team debated for 20 minutes whether it was genius before realizing the model had crashed mid-generation. The blank canvas was accidentally shipped to the client, who loved it.

cards.headline: NotebookLM by Google — Your AI Research Partner
cards.body: Upload PDFs, docs, or URLs and NotebookLM builds a personal AI that only answers from your sources — no hallucinations from the open web. The audio overview feature turns your documents into a podcast-style discussion. Perfect for research-heavy work.

cards.headline: Top AI Skills in Demand (Apr 2026)
cards.body: Source: LinkedIn AI Jobs Report, April 2026
Open source is not just an alternative to proprietary AI — it's becoming the default. The future belongs to models everyone can inspect, improve, and trust.
cards.author: Yann LeCun
